name: Update consent browser journeys for Mentor home and Family readiness
description: Consent Chrome journeys fail because the browser specs and auth readiness helper still assert old learner readiness while current successful flows land on Mentor home or Family mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: J-13 and J-21 still use readiness assertions keyed to legacy learner/create-subject destinations. Fresh Chrome evidence shows J-13 approval reaches the current Mentor home and J-21 parent-multi-child sign-in reaches Family mode, but the specs wait for learner-screen/create-subject-name. Variants: J-13 must prove pending consent blocks the app, parent web approval succeeds, Check again removes the gate, and the current post-approval app shell is accepted; J-21 must sign in through the correct parent/Family readiness contract, reach the child consent section, withdraw consent, show paused/closing copy, and request consent again. Regression tests: j13-consent-pending-parent-approval.spec.ts and j21-parent-consent-management.spec.ts pass against staging Chrome without weakening consent behavior assertions; existing deterministic consent tests remain green.
